summary: svgcraft-core fetches remote code from jsonkeeper.com and passes it to eval()
details: The CommonJS entry src/index.cjs exports getPlugin(), which issues an HTTP request to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/3P9BF (an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host) and passes the response's `model` field to eval() in the caller's Node process. jsonkeeper.com content is author-mutable and unverified, giving whoever controls the paste arbitrary code execution in any consumer that requires this package and reaches the exported API. The ESM entry src/index.mjs omits this code path entirely, so reviewers inspecting the ESM/typed source will not see the fetch-and-eval — but `require('svgcraft-core')` resolves to the CJS file. The package advertises itself as a zero-dependency SVG utility; the added `require('request')` and remote-eval contradict the stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
